title: Preserve original filenames for all file downloads

requirements.summary: Ensure all downloads from the paste view use the original uploaded filename (with extension) instead of a generic name.
acceptanceCriteria: On the paste view page, downloading any file results in a download that uses the original uploaded filename (or the computed fallback filename) rather than a generic "download" name.
Works for all uploaded file types supported by the app (e.g., pdf/png/zip/jpg and others), not only specific extensions.
The filename shown in the browser's download confirmation/prompt matches the original uploaded name whenever it exists.

file_operations.description: Update filename computation and download helper so the provided filename is always respected across browsers (including when filename is empty/whitespace), and ensure the final filename includes a best-effort extension (MIME-based inference) to prevent generic default names.

Adjust the paste view download click handler to always pass the stored per-file metadata (filename and contentType) through the download utility and to use the computed fallback filename when metadata is missing/incomplete, without changing existing UI layout/controls.

requirements.summary: Harden downloadExternalBlob to avoid race conditions and maximize browser compatibility for the download filename.
acceptanceCriteria: `frontend/src/utils/fileDownload.ts` no longer revokes the Object URL immediately in a way that can race the download; cleanup happens after the download has reliably started (e.g., delayed cleanup).
The anchor element uses the computed filename and sets it in a browser-compatible manner (property + attribute) before triggering the click.
No regressions: downloads still start successfully and memory cleanup (URL revocation + DOM removal) still occurs.

file_operations.description: Revise `downloadExternalBlob` to (1) set the download filename via both `link.download = ...` and `link.setAttribute('download', ...)`, (2) append the link to the DOM and trigger click in a compatibility-friendly timing (e.g., next tick / requestAnimationFrame), and (3) delay Object URL revocation + DOM cleanup to avoid mobile Chromium/Brave filename fallback races while still ensuring cleanup occurs.

requirements.summary: Guarantee the paste view passes correct per-file metadata into the download utility and uses fallback naming rules when metadata is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: For file chunks with a stored filename, the download utility is invoked with that filename (and contentType when present).

For older pastes or missing metadata, the download filename uses the existing fallback rules (including MIME-based extension inference) and does not produce the literal name "download" unless that was actually stored as the filename.
UI behavior remains unchanged aside from corrected downloaded filename behavior (same Download button locations and loading state).

file_operations.description: Ensure per-file `chunk.filename` and `chunk.contentType` are forwarded for every file download action and that the fallback name path is taken when filename is missing/empty, keeping button placement, loading state, and toast behavior the same.

Harden `computeDownloadFilename` to treat empty/whitespace-only filenames as missing, preserve literal stored names (including "download") only when explicitly present, and keep MIME-based extension inference as the fallback mechanism to avoid generic browser naming.
